perturbation.original_text: D. In the event of termination under this section, VIP will give BNL, at its request and E - 9 direction, such copies of BNL data maintained on the VIIP system in a format and in a manner as designated by BNL. BNL shall pay a fee to VIP for preparing such data. Such fee shall be $100 per hour for programming time and $150 per hour computer processing time.
perturbation.changed_text: D. In the event of termination under this section, VIP will give BNL, at its request and E - 9 direction, such copies of BNL data maintained on the VIIP system in a format and in a manner as designated by BNL. BNL shall pay a fee to VIP for preparing such data.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the specific rates for 'programming time' and 'computer processing time' creates an omission that introduces uncertainty about the fees BNL is required to pay. While BNL is still obligated to pay a fee for data preparation, the absence of defined rates makes enforcement ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: D. In the event of termination under this section, VIP will give BNL, at its request and E - 9 direction, such copies of BNL data maintained on the VIIP system in a format and in a manner as designated by BNL. BNL shall pay a fee to VIP for preparing such data.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the specific rates for 'programming time' and 'computer processing time' in the Changed Text directly contradicts the implied expectation of specific rates in the Contradicted Text. While the Contradicted Text states 'BNL shall pay a fee to VIP for preparing such data', the original text defined the rates which are now absent. This absence creates ambiguity and uncertainty about the fees BNL is required to pay upon termination, making the contract less enforceable. This directly aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' where essential language is removed, creating a contradiction.
